titleStmt.title: Letter from B. B. Cubitt, War Office, to Sir Matthew Nathan, 2 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Bertram B. Cubitt

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Bertram B. Cubitt (1862-1942), assistant under-secretary of State at the War Office to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), under-secretary for Ireland. A Mrs. E. Morgan has been tried for illegally drawing Army Separation Allowance and is now required to make restitution. This letter concerns a sum of £5 already recovered and the writer requests that this amount be remitted to the Regimental Paymaster in Dublin. For reference purposes, he supplies details regarding the soldier concerned in this case. A handwritten note in the margin, by assistant under-secretary for Ireland, Sir Edward O'Farrell (1857-1926), gives further accounting instructions.Cubitt was originally appointed to the War Office in 1886 and was under-secretary of state from 1914 until his retirement in 1926. Nathan was appointed under-secretary in 1914 but resigned after the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/20438

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
M.A
Any further communication on this
subject should be addressed toâ
The Secretary,
War Office,
London S.W.,
and the following number quoted.
46/M/629. Accounts 3. War Office, London, S.W. 2nd December, 1915. Sir, With reference to your letter No18367 of the
12th November, I am commanded by Army council to
request that you will be so good so to remit to the
Regimental Paymaster, Dublin, the sum of Â£5 recovered
from Mrs E. Morgan in respect of Separation Allowance
irregularly drawn by her. The official description of the soldier
concerned is No. 6926 Private J.Morgan, 4th Royal Irish
Rifles and it would assist the Regimental Paymaster
in dealing with the case if these particulars are
embodied in your letter. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, B B Cubitt Request Mr Moorehead
C.S. to forward cheque,
payable to the
Regimental Paymaster
Dublin, for Â£5
plus any sums
since paid in
by Mrs Morgan.
Inquire as to person
nominated to receive
her weekly payments
of 10/-
4/12/15 The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle, DUBLIN.
